title: Job-Order Cost Accounting: In job-order costing, manufacturing costs are first accumulated in a...
summary: Assess whether job-order costing uses one or multiple accounts for cost accumulation.
prompt: In job-order costing, manufacturing costs are first accumulated in a single account.
solution: In job-order costing, manufacturing costs are accumulated in multiple accounts: Raw Materials Inventory, Factory Labour, and Manufacturing Overhead, before being assigned to Work in Process Inventory.
